entries.title: What we're learning about suspected Washington press dinner gunman
entries.snippet: The man arrested at the event attended by President Trump is due in court on Monday.

entries.title: Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
entries.snippet: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

entries.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
entries.snippet: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

entries.title: Russian fighters confirm withdrawal from northern Mali city after separatist attacks
entries.snippet: Ethnic Tuareg fighters say they are in control of Kidal following a weekend of nationwide attacks, alongside Islamist groups.

entries.title: Michael Jackson biopic smashes box office record
entries.snippet: The film received poor reviews but has stormed the box office, taking $217m (£160m) globally.

entries.title: At least 42 killed in Chad after water well dispute escalates
entries.snippet: The dispute was initially between two families before escalating into a cycle of reprisal attacks.

entries.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
entries.snippet: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

entries.title: Pirates seize another vessel off Somali coast as threat level increased
entries.snippet: The UK's maritime monitor reports at least four suspected piracy incidents in the past week off the coast of Somalia.

entries.title: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit
entries.snippet: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church of England, had a private discussion with Pope Leo during the trip.

entries.title: French teen who licked vending machine straw faces jail in Singapore
entries.snippet: The teen, who posted on social media a video of himself licking the straw, could be jailed for more than two years.

entries.title: Missing 5-year-old girl likely abducted from Outback home, police say
entries.snippet: Police are searching for a man recently released from prison over her disappearance.

entries.title: Performer describes locking eyes with Trump as they ducked for cover during shooting
entries.snippet: Oz Pearlman - a self-described "mentalist" - was speaking to Trump, First Lady Melania Trump and press secretary Karoline Leavitt as the attack happened.

entries.title: The Chinese sports brand taking on Nike and Adidas
entries.snippet: Now one of the biggest sportswear firms, Anta's rise follows a playbook adopted by many Chinese giants.

entries.title: Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
entries.snippet: In a rare interview, Commander Robert Brovdi shared how his unit accounts for a third of all targets destroyed on the battlefield.

entries.title: Canada's Carney has enjoyed a long political honeymoon. Now comes the test
entries.snippet: Global acclaim and domestic political gains have defined Mark Carney's first year as Canada's PM. He now faces pressure to deliver on his bold promises.

entries.title: Remembering Raghu Rai: The photographer who showed India to itself
entries.snippet: With Rai's passing, India has lost a visual historian who was a chronicler of its most defining and often painful moments.

entries.title: Sawe smashes two-hour mark to 'move goalposts for marathon running'
entries.snippet: Sabastian Sawe makes history at the London Marathon as the first person to run a sub-two-hour marathon in a competitive race.

entries.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
entries.snippet: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

entries.title: Video shows correspondents' dinner suspect charge checkpoint
entries.snippet: Surveillance video released by Donald Trump shows the suspect running past metal detectors as security agents draw their guns.

entries.title: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
entries.snippet: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

entries.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
entries.snippet: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

entries.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
entries.snippet: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.

entries.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy, as Trump insists U.S. has 'the cards'
entries.snippet: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

entries.title: Inflation is sucking the life out of teacher pay raises, report says
entries.snippet: A new review of state education data shows teacher pay increases can't keep up with inflation and fewer students are enrolled in public schools.

entries.title: Alleged White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter set to appear in federal court
entries.snippet: The suspected gunman will face charges, including assault on a federal officer using a dangerous weapon.

entries.title: Scientists search the microbiome for clues to the rise in colorectal cancers
entries.snippet: Unlike many cancers, colorectal cancer has become more lethal for people at younger ages. Doctors are sleuthing out why.

entries.title: Musk vs. Altman: Tech CEOs head to court over the fate of OpenAI
entries.snippet: The former OpenAI business partners are embroiled in a high-stakes dispute over the future of one of the world's top AI companies.

entries.title: Climate disaster victims are rebuilding using prefab homes from boxy to bespoke
entries.snippet: Wildfires, hurricanes, tornadoes and floods fueled by manmade climate change are changing the housing industry. That's because people are embracing prefab homes that can withstand extreme weather.

entries.title: Ingenious? Orwellian? Or both? Supreme Court considers constitutionality of 'geofence' warrants
entries.snippet: The technique allows police to tap into giant tech-firm databases to find out who was near the scene of a crime and may have been involved.

entries.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
entries.snippet: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

entries.title: Justice Department cites Correspondents' Dinner shooting in push to drop Trump ballroom lawsuit
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump's Justice Department is using the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday to try to pressure preservationists to drop their lawsuit over his planned $400 million ballroom on the site of the former East Wing of the White House.

entries.title: At least 2 dead after tornadoes destroy homes in northern Texas
entries.snippet: A tornado-producing thunderstorm left at least two people dead in northern Texas and displaced at least 20 families, with many homes sustaining major damage, authorities said Sunday.

entries.title: Palestinian authorities call local elections in a central Gaza community and the occupied West Bank a success
entries.snippet: Palestinian authorities said Sunday that local elections in a single Gaza community and the Israeli-occupied West Bank were a success and called them a step toward a long-delayed presidential election in the territories and eventual statehood.

entries.title: Colombia bus bombing death toll rises to 20 during a wave of violence
entries.snippet: The attack happened Saturday when an explosive device was detonated on a bus traveling along the Pan-American Highway in the municipality of Cajibio.

entries.title: Malian defense minister killed in attack as jihadi and rebel forces seized towns and military bases
entries.snippet: Mali's defense minister was killed in a sweeping attack by jihadis and rebels who seized several towns and military bases, authorities said Sunday.

entries.title: Strikes in Ukraine and Russia kill at least 16 on Chernobyl's 40th anniversary
entries.snippet: Strikes across Ukraine, Russian-occupied territory and Russia over the past day killed at least 16 people, authorities said, as the Chernobyl anniversary highlights nuclear risks of the Russia-Ukraine war.

entries.title: What to know about the world's largest art heist from a Boston museum
entries.snippet: For decades, the 1990 theft of 13 artworks from the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum — now valued at more than $500 million — has remained unsolved.

entries.title: Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect wrote about grievances against Trump and targeting administration officials
entries.snippet: The man accused of opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner railed against Trump administration policies and referred to himself as a "Friendly Federal Assassin" in writings sent to family members minutes before an attack.

entries.title: Trump calls for unity and bipartisan healing after another violent incident
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump was somberly contemplative and unusually conciliatory after confronting what he saw as a third attempt on his life in less than two years.

entries.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner attack suspect is tutor and computer programmer from California
entries.snippet: Social media posts that appear to match the California man arrested Saturday in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner show he is a highly educated tutor and amateur video game developer.

entries.title: WATCH: D.C. officials share updates on the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
entries.snippet: The suspect in a shooting incident at Saturday's correspondents' dinner attended by President Donald Trump and other senior U.S. officials was carrying guns and knives and was believed to be acting alone, the mayor of Washington said.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump holds news conference after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
entries.snippet: Trump said the suspect was armed with multiple weapons before stopped by Secret Service. One officer was shot, but he was protected by a bulletproof vest.

entries.title: Trump unharmed after security incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
entries.snippet: A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside a high-profile journalists' dinner attended by President Donald Trump and multiple senior U.S. leaders on Saturday night. The president was uninjured and was hustled away.

entries.title: Police officer killed in Chicago hospital shooting identified, second officer in critical condition
entries.snippet: A Chicago officer who was fatally shot by a man receiving treatment at a hospital while in police custody has been identified as 38-year-old John Bartholomew, authorities said.

entries.title: Mexico says 2 U.S. federal agents who died were not authorized to participate in any local operation
entries.snippet: Mexico's government said Saturday that two U.S. federal agents recently killed in a car crash in the country's northern region were not authorized to participate in operations in Mexico.

entries.title: U.S. doctors warn of a potentially bad year for tick-borne diseases
entries.snippet: Tick season seems to be off to a fast start, with an unusually high number of bites already reported across the country.

entries.title: What to know if your flight is canceled as jet fuel costs rise
entries.snippet: With airlines worldwide canceling flights as the war in the Middle East strains jet fuel supplies and pushes up prices, here's what to know if your flight is canceled.

entries.title: Wildfires in southeastern Georgia continue to threaten residents
entries.snippet: Two wildfires in southeastern Georgia continued to threaten homes and lives on Saturday as officials warned that strong winds could spread the flames.

entries.title: Analysis: 'Just war' has guided Catholic thinking on conflict for centuries – including criticism of Iran war
entries.snippet: A growing choir of Catholic voices has criticized the Iran conflict by invoking the concept of "just war" – an evolving tradition that has guided Christian thinking about war and peace for 1,500 years.

entries.title: Latest ceasefire talks fail as Iran's top diplomat leaves Pakistan and Trump tells envoys not to go
entries.snippet: The latest ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran appeared to have failed Saturday after Tehran's top diplomat left Pakistan and President Trump said he told envoys not to go.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: US news: Suspect in DC gala shooting to appear in court
entries.snippet: A suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner is set to be formally charged in court. Plus, Britain's King Charles III is headed to the US after a security review cleared the planned trip.

entries.title: Will the Iran war end Strait of Hormuz oil supremacy?
entries.snippet: The conflict has brutally exposed the energy market's Achilles' heel. While global powers China, India and the EU push renewables, Gulf leaders are advancing plans for new bypass pipelines to safeguard their oil empires.

entries.title: Musk vs. OpenAI trial to get underway
entries.snippet: Two giants in the AI sector are set for a courtroom battle: Elon Musk (xAI) is taking on Sam Altmann (OpenAI). Musk has accused Altmann of betraying OpenAI's originally nonprofit mission.

entries.title: Portugal's arms industry is taking off
entries.snippet: Portugal has built up a surprisingly large, independent arms industry over the past five years. But whether it can be truly autonomous with the US as a major ally remains to be seen.

entries.title: Germany news: Rise in young people refusing military service
entries.snippet: Amid talk of a possible return of compulsory military service, more young Germans are applying for conscientious objector status. Meanwhile, Germany has become the world's fourth-largest military spender. DW has more.

entries.title: India news: Delhi to get respite, heat wave persists in other parts
entries.snippet: Storms are expected in the capital, but severe heat across multiple states would continue, India's weather department said. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Iran war deadline heats up Trump-Congress showdown
entries.snippet: A US president can order a military operation without congressional approval, but only for 60 days. After that they must seek formal approval from Congress. For the Iran war, this deadline expires on May 1.

entries.title: Iran's Araghchi visits Russia as talks with US on hold
entries.snippet: Iran's foreign minister is continuing his diplomatic drive amid the war against his country with a trip to Russia. It comes after planned talks with the US in Pakistan were canceled.

entries.title: SIPRI: Record arms spending, again
entries.snippet: Global military spending hit a record high in 2025. The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute, or SIPRI, warns the trend is likely to continue — and carries additional risks.

entries.title: Russia, North Korea agree 'long-term' military cooperation
entries.snippet: Russia's defense minister and the chairman of the State Duma are both visiting North Korea. One helped inaugurate a memorial to North Korean soldiers killed fighting Ukraine, the other hashed out a new defense deal.

entries.title: Colombia highway bomb attack kills at least 19
entries.snippet: Suspected guerillas carried out a deadly explosives attack on the Pan-American Highway in southwest Colombia. The region has been gripped by violence over the past few days.

entries.title: Gunman was likely targeting Trump team, says official
entries.snippet: The suspect in the attempted attack at the White House Correspondents' dinner is believed to have been targeting US President Donald Trump and members of his administration.

entries.title: Syria: First trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
entries.snippet: A court in Damascus is trying one official in former President Bashar Assad's government in person, and several more in absentia. Assad and his brother are among the defendants. Both are thought to have fled to Russia.

entries.title: Germany pensions will fall short of living standards: Merz
entries.snippet: Germany's state pension will only give "basic cover" in retirement, Chancellor Friedrich Merz has warned amid hot debate. How does the system compare globally?

entries.title: Germany news: Söder calls for compulsory military service
entries.snippet: The Bavarian state premier has criticized the government's voluntary plan. Meanwhile, Chancellor Friedrich Merz condemned the shooting in Washington.

entries.title: Sawe smashes 2-hour mark setting record at London Marathon
entries.snippet: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history in London as the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He beat the world record by more than a minute.

entries.title: Mali: Fighting continues as Tuareg separatists claim Kidal
entries.snippet: A spate of attacks was coordinated by Tuareg separatists and jihadis with links to al-Qaeda. Among them, a car bomb in Bamako killed Mali's defense minister.

entries.title: Queer as folk: The gay scandal that shook the German Empire
entries.snippet: Germany's last emperor, a gilded porcelain vase and a general who had a heart attack in a pink tutu: The Eulenburg Affair shook the German Empire to its core and paved the way for the downfall of the monarchy.

entries.title: India news: Modi targets TMC in Bengal rally
entries.snippet: PM Modi addressed rallies in West Bengal, taking jibes at the opposition party TMC ahead of the second phase of the state poll. Meanwhile, fresh clashes have erupted in Manipur.
